notes: Audio crackles get increasingly worse until another sound card is chosen (buffer issue?) and controls often lock up, press false keys or lose track of the mouse during combat scenarios. Otherwise everything seems to be running as if native.

title: Star Trek Online
notes: Slightly more stutters than Windows version.  Did not work with driver 390.67, but starts fine on 390.87.  First time you log in it takes a very long time to start (if the bar fills it is working).  Fullscreen Windowed mode seems to work best.  It may be necessary to start in safe mode to reset the graphics options.  Tested for 30 minutes.

title: Star Trek Online
notes: Nothing needed for it to work, runs great

title: Star Trek Online
notes: Choose safe login if game times out connecting to servers, only had to do this on first launch.

gpu: X.Org Radeon RX 580 Series (POLARIS10, DRM 3.26.0, 4.18.0-sabayon, LLVM 6.0.1)
notes: Launcher works fine, the game launches and plays without issue though it will stutter a bit without the poe stutter fix.
title: Star Trek Online

notes: Everything works. After hours of play, you may start to hear sound glitching. Restarting the game makes it go away.
title: Star Trek Online

notes: Performance is fine and everything looks perfect, but panning with the mouse has a several second lag that makes actually playing the game impossible.
title: Star Trek Online

notes: Did not launch.  Did not try any fixes (yet).
title: Star Trek Online

notes: Launcher runs and updates. Engage button causes Launcher freeze.
title: Star Trek Online

notes: Launcher runs and updates. Click Engage button causes Launcher freeze until killed
title: Star Trek Online

notes: When panning/looking around with the mouse, there's a delay that causes the look to be slow and sort of "drag" and be delayed. By looking at evhz's output (evhz-git from the AUR), it appears to be some sort of polling issue. Moving the mouse around normally yields 1000hz polling rates, which is apparently good. However, when panning, it drops to 125-300hz which increases polling latency, and would explain this issue. Other than that, performance seems better than Windows on maximum settings.
title: Star Trek Online

notes: Loading times during the Cryptic Studios and Age of Discovery splash can sometimes be a little long (and will very rarely will ctd at the splash). Otherwise runs perfectly.
title: Star Trek Online

notes: The game works perfectly for me.  No Problems found.
title: Star Trek Online

cpu: AMD A10-7700K APU with Radeon R7 Graphics
notes:
I had to add the startup launch option to steam(under properties on steam library- you can right click the title in library to pull it up):  PROTON_USE_WINED3D=1 %command%

I had slow loading but no slower than windows.
title: Star Trek Online

notes: At frist, launcher would just crash shortly after it opened. Disabling D3d11 fixed the crash, but launcher would freeze when clicking "engage" and needed to be killed.
title: Star Trek Online

notes: Launcher works. When using Wine D3d11, the launcher doesn't freeze when clicking engage. However the game crashes during loading with the following error during the loading phase: "shadercachemanager/shadercachemanager.cpp (306) : Assertion Failed: nHitCount <= 1"
title: Star Trek Online

notes: Updating to 3.16-5 fixed the game launch for me. It's playable, but sometimes freezes for up to a second when moving the camera.
title: Star Trek Online

notes: The Launcher works, but after pressing engage he freezes. The tweaks does'nt work for me.
